title: ALP Diagnostic Document v1
description: Validates the versioned machine diagnostics document produced by `alp validate --format json` (scripts/alp_cli/diagnostic_format.py:to_machine_json) -- the IDE/LSP/CI-facing contract for a validation run, replacing terminal-prose parsing. `render()` in scripts/alp_cli/diagnostic.py (the Rust-style human renderer, --format human) is UNCHANGED by this contract; this schema governs only the machine document. Ranges are zero-based (LSP `Position`/`Range` convention: line 0 is the first line, character 0 is the first column) -- the SDK's internal `Diagnostic.line`/`.col` are 1-based (Rust-style) and are converted on export, never mutated in place. SARIF 2.1.0 export (to_sarif) is a SEPARATE artefact governed by the OASIS SARIF schema, not this one -- SARIF `region` is 1-based by spec, the opposite convention, so the two exporters must not share range values. Stability: schemaVersion 1 is additive-only (new optional fields only); a breaking change ships as schemaVersion 2. Consumers MUST check `schemaVersion` (the version/capability handshake) and reject documents whose value they do not understand before parsing further.

schemaVersion.description: Version/capability handshake. A consumer that only understands schemaVersion 1 MUST reject (not best-effort-parse) any document with a different value.

version.description: The emitting tool's own version (scripts/alp_cli.__version__), NOT the schemaVersion.

uri.description: The file the diagnostic applies to (a path or file:// URI).

severity.description: LSP DiagnosticSeverity mapping for IDE/LSP consumers: error=1 (Error), warning=2 (Warning), note=3 (Information). There is no LSP 4 (Hint) equivalent emitted by this tool today. SARIF `level` mapping (to_sarif, separate artefact): error->"error", warning->"warning", note->"note" (direct 1:1, SARIF has a native "note" level).

code.description: The stable diagnostic code, e.g. ALP-B005 (see metadata/error-catalog.json).

documentationUri.description: Link to the code's landing page (docs/diagnostics/<code>.md), mirroring Diagnostic.doc_url.
